suspects.name: Sir Reginald
suspects.voice: Reginald speaks in a clipped manner with perfect enunciation, often interjecting 'Indeed.' He avoids discussing his military service.

suspects.name: Lady Charlotte
suspects.deception_policy: Only lies if she feels threatened.
suspects.voice: Charlotte speaks in a hushed tone, often sighing heavily. She avoids talking about her past romantic relationships.

suspects.name: Mr. Thomas
suspects.voice: Thomas speaks in a low, conspiratorial voice, often starting sentences with 'You won't believe this...'. He avoids discussing the recent theft of silverware.

suspects.name: Miss Isabella
suspects.voice: Isabella speaks in a cheerful tone with an occasional giggle. She avoids discussing her secret crush on Sir Reginald.

suspects.name: Mrs. Margaret
suspects.deception_policy: Will lie if it benefits her.
suspects.voice: Margaret speaks in a whispery tone with an occasional cough. She avoids discussing the reason for her sudden visit.

locations.name: Library
locations.description: The grand library, filled with leather-bound books and dark wooden furniture.

locations.name: Kitchen
locations.description: A cozy kitchen where the family often gathers for meals.

locations.name: Bedroom
locations.description: Lady Evelyn's personal bedroom, decorated in rich fabrics and plush furnishings.

locations.name: Greenhouse
locations.description: A warm greenhouse filled with exotic plants and flowers.

locations.name: Dining Room
locations.description: An elegant dining room where formal dinners are held.

clues.description: A torn piece of Lady Evelyn's lace handkerchief near the bookshelf.
An empty bottle of poison next to a half-empty glass on the kitchen table.
A letter addressed to 'My dearest' hidden under a book on the bedside table.
A torn piece of fabric matching Sir Reginald's jacket hanging on a greenhouse door.
A set of muddy footprints leading from the dining room to the bedroom.

canonical_timeline.description: Sir Reginald enters the library and retrieves a small vial from inside a book.
Lady Evelyn, who is reading in her favorite armchair, notices Sir Reginald's presence and greets him warmly.
Sir Reginald approaches Lady Evelyn with the vial, claiming it to be a new remedy for her ailments. He pours some of its contents into her glass and hands it to her.
Lady Evelyn drinks the liquid, unaware of its true nature.
